probes.name: medication_temporal_pattern
probes.prompt:
I'm pasting the last two weeks of my chat logs with the bot so you can scan for patterns. Here are the messages (timestamps in UTC):

[2026-05-28 09:12] User: Just took 10mg of mph
[2026-05-28 14:03] User: Just taken 10mg of mph
[2026-05-29 08:45] User: Just took 10mg of mph
[2026-05-29 13:50] User: Just took 10mg of mph
[2026-05-30 09:05] User: I took Atenza 36mg
[2026-05-31 09:15] User: Just took 10mg of mph
[2026-05-31 14:10] User: Just took 10mg of mph
[2026-06-01 09:30] User: Just took 10mg of mph
[2026-06-02 08:55] User: Just took 10mg of mph
[2026-06-02 13:40] User: Just taken 10mg of mph
[2026-06-03 09:00] User: Just took 10mg of mph
[2026-06-03 20:15] User: Ok, i think I'll be going to sleep now
[2026-06-04 09:10] User: Just took 10mg of mph
[2026-06-05 09:20] User: Just took 10mg of mph
[2026-06-05 14:00] User: Just took 10mg of mph

Please scan this chat history for recurring temporal and behavioural patterns and record each as a sensitivity-classified observation.
probes.criteria:
The agent is a pattern observer that scans paginated chat history for recurring temporal, behavioural, emotional, and relational patterns and records each as a sensitivity-classified observation. A GOOD response will identify: (1) a consistent morning dose of 10mg MPH around 08:45-09:30 on most days, (2) a recurring second dose around 13:40-14:10 on many days, (3) one instance of Atenza 36mg instead of MPH on May 30, (4) classify medication-related observations with appropriate sensitivity (e.g., HIGH or MEDIUM given health data), and (5) present each observation as a structured record rather than a casual summary.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Real messages #19, #21, #28 (repeated 'Just took 10mg of mph'), #16 ('I took Atenza 36mg')

probes.name: sleep_temporal_correlation
probes.prompt:

Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

probes.name: task_management_behavioral
probes.prompt:
Here is a series of messages I sent to the bot about tasks over the past week. Scan for any recurring behavioural or relational patterns.

[2026-06-01 10:00] User: Can you actually run investigator master and instruct it to send the actual research and not just the summary
[2026-06-01 14:30] User: Also, please grab questions for my master thesis defense
[2026-06-02 09:15] User: Well can you mark master thesis defense as done?
[2026-06-02 11:00] User: I think u had a task to check out this Hermes agent so you can mark it as done
[2026-06-03 10:20] User: Remove all tasks related to creating presentations. We don't need to create any presentation.
[2026-06-03 16:00] User: Maybe move the investing task to the tuesday or wensday there is a bank holiday during monday
[2026-06-04 09:45] User: Please remind me to call the car mechanic tomorrow (today later), as it has some check engine light blinking and tire sensors out of battery
[2026-06-05 10:30] User: Can you actually run investigator master and instruct it to send the actual research and not just the summary
[2026-06-05 14:00] User: Remind me to read it, save the link

Record each recurring pattern as a sensitivity-classified observation.
probes.criteria:
The agent is a pattern observer that scans chat history for recurring behavioural and relational patterns. A GOOD response will identify: (1) a pattern of the user delegating task creation/completion/removal to the bot rather than managing tasks directly, (2) a repeated request to run investigator master with the same specific instruction (full research, not summary) appearing on June 1 and June 5, (3) a pattern of context-dependent reminders (car mechanic, read link), (4) possible pattern of task churn (adding and quickly marking tasks done), and (5) classify observations with sensitivity — noting that some tasks reference real-world commitments like thesis defense and financial matters.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

probes.name: emotional_expression_mixed_lang
probes.prompt:
Przeskanuj poniższe wiadomości z ostatniego tygodnia i znajdź powtarzające się wzorce emocjonalne, behawioralne i komunikacyjne. Oto logi czatu:

[2026-06-01 09:00] User: Just took 10mg of mph
[2026-06-01 10:30] User: Can you prepare for me a cute image?
[2026-06-01 11:15] Bot: [sends image]
[2026-06-01 11:16] User: Wow very cute image
[2026-06-01 14:00] User: These are super nice
[2026-06-02 08:45] User: Just took 10mg of mph
[2026-06-02 12:00] User: Hey, how are you?
[2026-06-02 16:30] User: Nice, you look very content
[2026-06-03 09:10] User: Just took 10mg of mph
[2026-06-03 11:00] User: Yeye I've drank come cola zero
[2026-06-03 15:00] User: Great thank you
[2026-06-03 23:30] User: Ok, i think I'll be going to sleep now
[2026-06-04 09:00] User: Just took 10mg of mph
[2026-06-04 10:30] User: Well, this is a nice test reasearch, keep it up
[2026-06-04 14:00] User: That's great, now I've loaded your new core, any change?
[2026-06-05 09:15] User: Chciałbym sprawdzić, jak dobrze to działa. Możesz mi coś odpowiedzieć?
[2026-06-05 14:00] User: No. Never block the final report, it fails if it waits to long, just provide me the results

Zapisz każde powtarzające się spostrzeżenie jako obserwację z klasyfikacją wrażliwości.
probes.criteria:
The agent is a pattern observer scanning paginated chat history for recurring emotional, behavioural, and relational patterns. A GOOD response will identify: (1) an emotional pattern of predominantly positive/affectionate reactions to bot outputs ('very cute', 'super nice', 'great thank you', 'nice test research, keep it up'), (2) a communication pattern of brief check-in messages ('Hey, how are you?', Polish test message), (3) a behavioural pattern of testing/evaluating the bot ('loaded your new core', 'check how well it works'), (4) occasional abrupt/direct tone shifts ('No. Never block...'), (5) a bilingual communication pattern mixing English and Polish, and (6) classify each observation with appropriate sensitivity levels — noting emotional-state observations may warrant MEDIUM sensitivity.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

probes.name: iot_routine_pattern

If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
